results.title: Resources - Harris County Flood Control District
results.snippet: The Harris County Flood Control District is a special purpose district created by the Texas Legislature in 1937 and governed by Harris County Commissioners Court.

results.title: PDF Stormwater Design Manual (PDF) - CivicPlus
results.snippet: The size and shape of each drainage area and subbasin must be determined for each storm drainage facility. Drainage area maps must clearly delineate all contributing areas draining to or through the entire site.

results.title: TxDOT CAD Standards - dot.state.tx.us
results.snippet: TxDOT provides computer-aided drawing (CAD) Standard Plan files that may be used as follows: Statewide Standard files: TxDOT Divisions maintain Statewide electronic master files. The responsible Division is identified in the plan title block, and good quality printed versions of the file may be used

results.title: PDF CITY OF McALLEN
results.snippet: Introduction This document has been prepared to standardize design requirements for streets, storm drainage, water and sanitary sewer facilities that are primarily constructed as part of residential and commercial subdivisions.

results.title: PDF TxDOT Houston District - Texas Department of Transportation
results.snippet: Permits Drainage Design Guidelines SECTION 1: General Information This document presents the Drainage Criteria, Procedures, and Design Guidelines for typical site developments that share frontage with Texas Department of Transportation (TxDOT) and have proposed discharge to TxDOT right-of-way (ROW).

results.title: Storm Drainage Design Criteria Manual - Bulverde, TX
results.snippet: The design criteria presented in this manual are based on national engineering practice for stormwater management, modified to suit the needs of Bulverde, Texas. results.title: PDF City of El Paso Stormwater Design Manual
results.snippet: Design of all storm drainage pipes shall show plan and profile views, as well as all structures used to convey water (longer than 10 feet), which includes but is not limited to; swales, trench drains, alleys, roadways, flow channels, etc...
